title: Not all internal state accessible through rootp
body:
Hi,

I'm picking up some legacy code that uses hierarchical access to internal member state.  I've found the relevant note about the interface changes in v4.210 and have added `->rootp` liberally throughout the code, but there are some paths that don't seem to be accessible and I don't understand why there are differences between the modules.

As an example, I have a register that was previously accessed via a path such as:

```
top->rootp->top__DOT__a__DOT__b__DOT__c__DOT__d__DOT__e
```

but what I can see now is that in `top___024root.h` I have a number of sub classes listed in the form:

```
VL_MODULE(Vtop___024root) {
    public:
        // CELLS
        Vtop_c* __PVT__top__DOT__a__DOT__b__DOT__c;
```

Looking at all the sub classes, they all seem to be for modules that are instanced more than once.  Is that the difference?  Did this change in v4.x as well?
